gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the Databricks storage benchmark job ownership inversion and found reverse-wrapper compatibility gaps. The wrapper now uses cloned document functions with an isolated globals dictionary, preserves legacy validator and private cluster-helper monkeypatch behavior, uses an honest legacy config subclass for direct construction and pickle support, preserves slotted layout, and has final re-review approval with no remaining findings.

issues.resolution: Changed the legacy wrapper to run cloned document functions with an isolated globals dictionary rather than mutating document module globals during the call.
issues.summary: Document-namespace monkeypatches could leak into legacy calls.

issues.resolution: Kept a legacy config subclass whose __post_init__ resolves legacy module globals for UC-root validation and StorageBenchmarkConfig construction.
issues.summary: Legacy-side validator monkeypatches were not observed by direct legacy config construction.

issues.resolution: Overlaid the legacy private cluster helper into the isolated namespace and added regression coverage.
issues.summary: Legacy private cluster-helper monkeypatches were not observed by legacy payload building.

issues.resolution: Kept the legacy config subclass under the legacy module path, added pickle coverage, and declared __slots__ = () to preserve the old slotted layout.
issues.summary: The compatibility config subclass initially had a misleading module identity and a non-slotted instance layout.

scope: document Databricks storage benchmark job implementation ownership
legacy Databricks storage benchmark job compatibility wrapper
legacy module execution and helper monkeypatch migration hooks
document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
repository secret scan over README, package metadata, source, tests, Databricks helpers, PR evidence, and GitHub metadata
GPT-5.5 review and re-review after resolving all compatibility findings
what_changed: Moved the Databricks storage benchmark job implementation into document_kv_cache.databricks_storage_benchmark_job.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.databricks_storage_benchmark_job into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.

Preserved legacy module execution, direct writer monkeypatch behavior, public and private helper monkeypatch behavior, pickle support, slotted config layout, and the previous legacy star-import surface.
Added document_kv_cache.storage.is_real_uc_volume_root to the document storage public surface.
Recorded document ownership in public package tests and package READMEs.
why: The generalized package should own the Databricks storage benchmark job entry point because storage-reader evidence is part of the V1 memory, disk, and Unity Catalog support story. Keeping legacy compatibility makes this an incremental, behavior-preserving step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
